observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

a. GSO and Accounting Office to establish stricter review and approval
process to verify asset classification before including the items in their
respective reports;

b. GSO to ensure that AREs and PCs are updated for all assets, not just on
newly acquired items. A reconciliation process should be institutionalized
between the GSO and Accounting Office to align property records with
financial reports; and

c. GSO and Municipal Accountant to make a final reconciliation of their
respective records and avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account
balances, strictly in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006,
particularly on the disposition and derecognition of non-existing/missing
PPE and adjust the books accordingly, in order to come up with reliable
PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and
accountability.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:

a. Demand immediate settlement/liquidation of the outstanding CAs,
especially from all AOs and employees still connected with the LGU,
pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the grant, utilization
and liquidation of CAs and avoid granting additional CAs unless the
previous CA is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made;

c. Institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring
AOs/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules
and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of CAs, if
warranted; and

d. Request to the Commission on Audit for write-off and/or adjustment for
dormant or non-moving account as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-
005. The request should be duly supported by the documents required in the
said Circular.

We recommended that Management:

a. Promptly submit the required RPCI to the Office of the Auditor for
verification;

b. Require the GSO to maintain and regularly update the SC for procured
supplies and materials. Likewise, GSO to maintain an updated RegSPI and
to submit weekly reports of semi-expendable property issued to the
accounting unit, serving as the basis for preparing JEV; and

c. Lastly, to submit the SSMI to the accounting unit as the primary basis for
recognizing the appropriate expense account.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to:
    i. Thoroughly review the accuracy and truthfulness of the RCDs of the
    collectors to ensure that they remit their collections to her, intact and
    in full amount within the prescribed period; and
    ii. Ensure that all her collections are deposited intact and in full amount,
    daily, the next banking day, at least once a week, or as soon as the
    collections reach ₱5,000.00, as applicable;

b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer and all AOs to prepare and immediately
submit their updated cashbooks to the Office of the Auditor. Henceforth,
prepare, maintain and regularly update their respective cashbooks, as well
as, regularly reconcile the same with the accounting records so that any
items for adjustment may be corrected and resolved promptly and the
amount of their respective accountabilities is accurate and ascertainable at
any given time;

c. Ensure that issued ORs by all AOs are sequentially and accurately dated,
and that all unused ORs are inventoried and monitored by the Treasurer’s
Office; and

d. Evaluate the accountability of concerned officers and institute appropriate
administrative or disciplinary actions for continued non-compliance.

We recommended that Management enforce strict adherence to bonding
requirements by ensuring that AOs are permitted to collect or manage public funds
only when covered by valid and continuous fidelity bonds.

We recommended that Management:

a. Continue its commitment to faithfully remit trust collections, however, the
current administration still has to exert effort in reconciling and
establishing the unremitted trust collections from prior years’ transactions
and once established, accordingly remit the same to the respective
government agencies; and

b. Instruct the Human Resource Department to immediately facilitate the
registration of all employees with PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG, and to provide
the Accounting Office with updated membership details.

We recommended that Management:

a. Comply with the timelines set forth in the aforementioned COA Circular
for submitting the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.
Likewise, promptly submit the year-end MDRRMF Report of Sources and
Utilization for CY 2024 to facilitate the timely verification of the accuracy
and propriety of the implemented PPAs;

b. Immediately submit the lacking supporting documents and require the
Accounting Office to refrain from processing DVs without complete
documentation; and

c. Lastly, refrain from charging against the LDRRMF those expenditures not
pertaining to disaster risk reduction and management or not in accordance
with the primary purpose for which the fund was established, otherwise,
these transactions will be disallowed in audit.

We reiterated that Management direct its GAD Focal Person to prepare a GPB
that can be implemented within the year and implement properly all planned GAD
activities to ensure that all GAD gender issues raised during the year are properly
addressed.

We recommended that Management require the concerned Officials to:

a. Submit all of the subsequently perfected LGU contract agreements,
together with their complete supporting documents, within five days from
the date of execution; and

b. Ensure that the contracts and payments are duly supported with complete
documentary requirements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to ensure that prescribed
planning and budgeting tools are prepared, submitted and effectively utilized and the
timelines in the planning and budgeting process are strictly observed as prescribed
under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure
that all DVs are supported by complete and proper documentation prior to the
processing and payment of any claim. Additionally, ensure that all suppliers and
service provider are duly registered with the PhilGEPS and maintain active
membership. Furthermore, strict compliance with Section 54. 3 of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 is likewise enjoined.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the concerned personnel to immediately submit the required missing
documents, subject for auditorial and contract review, for proper
documentation and audit validation; and

b. Strictly comply with the posting and reporting requirements under Section
5.0 of LBC No. 154 dated February 6, 2024 to ensure transparency,
accountability and timely assessment/monitoring in the Release and
Utilization of LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC projects.

We recommended that Management:

a. Review and evaluate the existing manpower resources to ensure adequate
staffing pattern that suit the present needs of the offices and create clear-
cut policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety
of hiring JOs/holders of COS;

b. Maximize the use of existing work force and resort to JO hiring only in the
exigency of the service and if it is not feasible for the LGU to hire said
services under a casual or contractual appointment;

c. Clearly define in the contract the nature of works or activities to be done by
the JOs/holders of COS; and

d. Require the submission of duly accomplished DTRs, quantified
accomplishment reports and definite proof of identification, to substantiate
actual work performed prior to the processing and disbursement of any
payments.

1.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

b. Reclassify the CIP account based on the date of completion instead

and recognize the corresponding depreciation expenses; and
d. Prepare adjusting entries to correct the under-provision of
depreciation expenses and recognize depreciation from the date of
completion which shall be for one month if the PPE is available for
use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month
if the PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month.

sanggunian.

7.11 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review

8.9 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to create an
Inventory Committee that will spearhead and monitor the implementation
of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE.

9.7 We recommended that the:
9.7.1 Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their
records to address the noted discrepancy of ₱23,091,282.01,
make the necessary adjustments and conduct the same
quarterly; and
9.7.2 Municipal Accountant submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements

10.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the

adjustments to arrive at their equal balances.

11.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit a
written request for write-off of the Advances to Officers and Employees

008.

12.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust the

13.7 We recommended that the:
13.7.1 Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to revert the
stale checks amounting to ₱700,133.04, and inform the Municipal
Treasurer for corresponding adjustment in her cashbook; and
13.7.2 Acting Municipal Treasurer: (a) inform the concerned payees that
their checks have become stale and that a request for replacement
is necessary for the issuance of a new check; and (b) to prevent

14.6 We recommended that the:
14.6.1 Liquidating Officer deposit the collections intact daily or not later
than the next banking day and refrain from using it for the LGU’s
immediate needs; and
14.6.2 Acting Municipal Treasurer monitor the compliance of the
Liquidating Officer, otherwise assume the responsibility to
deposit the collection.

15.7 We recommended and the payroll in-charge agreed to prioritize
immediate reconciliation with GSIS to address the outstanding balance
and implement a monitoring system to ensure that all future premium
payments are made on time and in full.

16.5 We recommended and the MHRMO agreed to establish a cut-off period
for all offices to ensure timely processing of payroll in order for the
Administrative Officer to facilitate timely remittance of premium and loan
payments to Pag-IBIG, in adherence to RA No. 9679 and its IRR.

17.10 We recommended that the:
17.10.1 Payroll-in-charge personnel withhold the monthly PhilHealth
premium contributions from the salaries of the Sangguniang
Bayan members and the Liga ng mga Barangay President; and
17.10.2 Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments and
remit the unwithheld premium contributions of the Sangguniang
Bayan members and the Liga ng mga Barangay President from
CY 2022 to 2024 to PhilHealth immediately to avoid the
imposition of penalties.

18.5 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to issue a directive to
all Implementing Units of the projects, programs and activities of the 20%
Development Fund to refrain from charging expenses or disbursements
that are administrative in nature to reduce, if not eliminate the risk of
recurrence.

19.6 We recommended that the:
19.6.1 MHRMO, in the processing of travel orders of the COS, require the
aforementioned certification, check alignment of the purpose of
the travel with the duties stipulated in contracts, and submit the
lacking contract of service of the identified COS worker; and
19.6.2 Municipal Accountant process travel claims that are duly
supported with the required certification and other necessary
supporting documents.

20.8 We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to refrain from
using the GAD Fund for expenses not related to addressing gender
issues, promoting gender equality, and providing opportunities for
women’s empowerment.

21.4 We recommended and the Local School Board thru the Focal Person
agreed to:
a. Document the provision of copies of the approved SEF Budget to the

b. Submit to the DepEd Central Office through the appropriate channels,
the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization or the SEF Budget
Accountability Form No. 1;
c. Furnish the Local Sanggunian of Placer and the Regional Office of
the DBM the same reports in No. 2 within the prescribed period; and
d. Post on the LGU's website and/or in at least three designated
conspicuous public places consistent with the deadline of the DILG
Full Disclosure Policy.

22.8.1 Acting Municipal Treasurer effect the available remedies for
collection of RPT and SET that have been long outstanding; and
22.8.2 Management allocate a budget for the massive land tax mapping
to be conducted by the Municipal Assessor to cleanse and update
their records and come up with an accurate list of RPT/SET
collectibles.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the concerned personnel to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government properties subjected to repairs;
Direct the GSO to maintain the records regarding the history of repairs of each of the properties of the LGU; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents, such as the pre-/post repair inspection reports, certificate of acceptance duly approved by the Local Chief Executive (LCE), Waste Materials Report and other documentary requirements, before processing claims for repair and maintenance.

Ensure that appropriate amount is included in the budget to cover for the registration and renewal fees and/or property insurance premiums of all motor vehicles and heavy equipment; and
Immediately facilitate the LTO registration and insurance of all serviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Adhere to the criteria in the development of infrastructure projects by taking into consideration the economic and social impact to the end users so that maximum and lasting benefits could be derived therefrom;
Conduct the required detailed engineering activities before prosecuting any infrastructure projects in accordance with Section 17.6, Rule VI of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
Consider the implementation “By Administration” of road opening, rehabilitation/regravelling and maintenance projects to save unnecessary costs and ensure economic and efficient use of government funds and resources.

Revisit the work and financial plan for the operation of the Placer Integrated Terminal and determine reason/s why until to date, the property remained unused/underutilized; and
Immediately convene the Management Board to explore measures to address identified issues/concerns for the full operation of the terminal and to maximize the benefits that the LGU and its constituents may derive therefrom.

The Rural Health Officer submit duly certified list of health workers assigned during the LGU vaccination activities that were granted the provisions of foods for further audit verification.

Funds were appropriated to implement COVID-19 related PPAs.

No Management action.

Henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer is reminded that charging of expense for every transaction must only be made when budget thereof is available, regular or supplemental appropriation ordinance, pursuant to budgetary laws and regulations.

Refrain from putting up the names and/or motto of government officials on all government PPAs and on procured goods in compliance with the aforecited regulations

The Municipal Accountant establish the RPT Receivable/SET Receivable with the corresponding SLs based on the duly Certified List to avoid misleading information in Annual Financial Report; and,

The Municipal Treasurer/ Disbursing Officer to provide an updated Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies to the Accountant.

Prepared JEV for adjustments to be made based on Certified Lists of Tax Payers.





Made a letter to Mun. Treasury Office to Provide an updated Certified Lists of Real Property Tax Delinquencies

Further, observe prudent and judicious use of government resources in the procurement of motor vehicles.

date of distribution/receipt by the beneficiary;
place/residence of the beneficiary; and,
Content of the relief goods as to the kind and number of items per pack.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The existing articles and provisions of the 2008 Revenue Code be revisited with the end-in-view of considering the call of the present times towards the best interest of the public and the government; and

Update the existing tax rates, fees and charges prescribed in the Revenue Code to increase the revenue collection of local taxes and be able to sustain an adequate, appropriate and relevant tax rates in relation to the Municipality’s fast-paced growth and development requirements.
observations.managementAction: No Management action taken.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended that the Management create an Inventory Committee
to conduct Physical Count and submit Report on Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment and direct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and Ledger Cards,
respectively. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the
total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

2.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review contents of
outstanding checks and coordinate with the Municipal Treasury that
they will be furnished with the list of checks staled and cancelled or
checks staled but reissued together with the original disbursement
vouchers and other supporting documents to facilitate accurate
preparation of journal entry vouchers and ensure proper recording of
the same.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Due
from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds Accounts to present fairly
the financial position of the municipality.

4.7 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
demand in writing from the concerned barangay LGUs on the
liquidation/settlement of their fund transfers received for fair
presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

5.4 We recommended that Management direct the Treasurer to return the
excess or unutilized fund from fully implemented projects to its source
agencies and instruct the Accountant to prepare the necessary journal
entries once an official receipt for the refund by the source agencies is
received to ensure fair presentation of the accounts in the FS.

6.6 We recommended that the LGU use the DPRI as the ceiling prices in the
determination of ABC in the procurement of drugs and medicines to
comply with the DOH Administrative Order No. 2019-0040 to achieve
the most advantageous prices.

7.11 We recommended that Management direct the concerned personnel to
procure all common-use supplies and materials to the PS-DBM to
strictly and religiously comply with the relevant laws, rules and
regulations taking into consideration the two (2) identified reasons
behind the non-compliance.

8.6 We recommended that Management ensure that cash advances of the
concerned accountable officers be liquidated in accordance with COA
Circular No. 97-002. Further, exhaust all possible remedies and demand
immediate liquidation of the foregoing unliquidated cash advances.

9.6 We recommended that Management set timelines to expedite the
remittances for PHIC and HDMF on or before the due dates fixed by the
foregoing provisions.
9.7 Further, reconcile the variances noted and settle any unremitted
premium/contributions from prior years.

10.7 We recommended that Management properly addressed the issues and
concerns that hindered the implementation of these projects so that
PPAs can all be implemented and carried out as planned.

11.5 We recommended that Management continue the implementation of
projects that promote and contribute to the economic development for
the benefit of its constituents.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
a. Direct the MGSO to practice the periodic reconciliation with the
accounting records to immediately detect errors/irregularities;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the PPE booked in the Trust
Fund to the General Fund books upon completion/acquisition. Also, ensure
monthly recognition of depreciation expense of all the PPE accounts and
record the adjustments in the government equity account for the prior
years’ depreciation expenses to reflect the correct book value of the PPE;
c. Direct the Inventory Committee to exert utmost effort in conducting the
activities relative to the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances; and
d. Require the Inventory Committee to immediately dispose those identified
unserviceable and obsolete PPE and drop from the books the book value
thereof in accordance with existing accounting and auditing rules and
regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
a. Prioritize issuance of demand letter to all AOs and employees to
immediately liquidate/settle their outstanding cash advances;
b. File a request for authority to write-off dormant or long outstanding
unliquidated cash advances, if warranted, to the Office of the Auditor; and
c. Continue to strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring
controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and full
accounting of balances and avoid undue accumulation of outstanding
cash advances.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Strictly monitor the conduct of regular and periodic verification, analysis
and validation of the existence of payable accounts to ensure that only
valid payables are recorded in the books; and
b. Prepare a certified schedule of aging of dormant payable accounts for
which there are no valid claimants/creditors and no supporting documents,
thereafter, revert the verified amounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of
the Government Equity Account by preparing the necessary Journal Entry
Vouchers as basis for recording in the books of accounts of the
Municipality.

We recommended that:
a. The LGU design its own proactive PPAs and provide funds therefor to
curtail and eliminate the deadly COVID-19 pursuant to existing laws and
issuances; and
b. The Rural Health Officer submit duly certified list of health workers
assigned during the LGU vaccination activities that were granted the
provisions of foods for further audit verification.

We recommended that the BAC must strictly evaluate the eligibility of all
bidders prior to its recommendation of award and the HOPE must exercise
utmost discretion in approving the award of contract. Only those
contractors duly licensed in their area of operations must be considered for
the government contract.

We recommended the following:
a. The LDRRM Office must formulate at least a 5-Year comprehensive and
integrated LDRRMP in accordance with the national, regional and
provincial framework, and policies on disaster risk reduction, to be
reviewed and approved by other regulatory agencies pursuant to RA No.
10121 which will serve as basis for the Annual Plan and Budget and submit
the same to the Local Legislative for adaption of Local DRRM Ordinance
of the LGU;
b. The LDRRM Council must monitor and evaluate the use and
disbursement of the LDRRM Fund based on the LDRRM Plan and
periodically advice the LDRRM Office on the status and progress of the
planned PPAs to avoid non-implementation of desirable disaster risk
reduction and management programs and activities;
c. Annual Plan must course through the PCVA tool and gender responsive
planning; and
d. The BAC and the Procuring Officer must exert effort to obtain prices most
advantageous to the LGU and thoroughly determine the qualification of
the potential supplier if permitted by its license to engage in sale of a
particular item.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the LSB to formulate a three-year program indicating strategic
prioritization policies for the allocation of SEF and subsequently prepare
and approve the Annual SEF Budget so that priority PPAs can be
implemented;
b. Ensure that Liquidated Damage is imposed on all procurement of goods
with delays in delivery equivalent to one tenth of one per cent of the cost of
the undelivered portion for every day of delay until actual delivery;
c. Instruct the person responsible for accepting deliveries to notify COA of
the time and date of the scheduled deliveries for ocular verification of the
existence and condition of supplies, materials and equipment and submit
copies of completely filled-out IARs within 24 hours upon acceptance; and
d. Require the supplier/contractor to post warranty security for all completed
and accepted goods and projects.

We recommended that Management:
a. Discontinue the practice of granting honoraria to the national government
officials/employees assigned in the Municipality until such time that all
additional allowances under RA No. 7305 are fully provided to its public
health workers; and
b. Adhere strictly to the requirements of DBM Budget Execution Guidelines
on the grant of additional allowance and other benefits to national
government officials/employees assigned to LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor and other LGU concerned
official, particularly the Municipal Budget Officer to submit explanation and proof of
legality on the donation granted to VMLP – Masbate Chapter, otherwise,
immediately refund the amount donated to the latter.
Henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer is reminded that charging of
expense for every transaction must only be made when budget thereof is available,
regular or supplemental appropriation ordinance, pursuant to budgetary laws and
regulations.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all PPAs are provided with
the prescribed tarpaulin signboards with complete and accurate data in
order to promote good governance and secure the right of the people to
information on matters of public concern; and
b. Refrain from putting up the names and/or motto of government officials on
all government PPAs and on procured goods in compliance with the
aforecited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant maintain Property Cards and Ledger Cards, respectively. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the Reports on the Physical Count on all PPEs of the LGU and to disclose the total Road Network System in the Notes to the Financial Statements. Likewise, we also recommend that management create a General Services Office, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all purchases of supplies as inventory and subsequently record it as expense upon distribution or consumption. Moreover, prepare necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the two accounts in the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat to post the required updated APP and awarded contracts and other documents in the GPPB Online Portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prohibit each Purchasing Department to request goods with reference to brand names. Instead, specifications should have to be the reference.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the project Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies between the cash accounts reported in SSAF and SRE. Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments on the omissions/errors to correct the reported balances of the affected accounts/balances in the books and SSAF/SRE, whenever applicable.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Project Accountant and concerned personnel to strictly observe the prescribed period in the submission of all accounting and project reports with complete supporting documents in the succeeding accounting period in compliance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan members prioritize the creation of the three (3) statutory position of the LDRRM Office in-charge of administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning as required by Section 12.b of the RA 10121 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Budget Limitations for PS of the LGU

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that cash advances of the concerned accountable officers be liquidated in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002. Further, exhaust all possible remedies and demand immediate liquidation of the foregoing unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 8

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management discontinue the practice of allowing deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending institutions from employees’ payroll other than those mandated by law. These personnel who contracted loans from these lending institutions must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors. Further, ensure that each employee receives net take home pay of at least ₱5,000.00 pursuant to the General Appropriations Act of 2020.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct those in charge of the payroll preparation and those in charge for the contribution of mandatory deductions to properly compute the Pag-IBIG premiums at ₱100.00 for employer’s share per employee. Further, strictly follow the maximum ceiling of Five Thousand Pesos (₱5,000.00) monthly to be used in the computation of employer share as provided under Section 7 of RA 9679, unless amended. Furthermore, for the meantime, stop paying for the employer share of the employees until such time that the excess payments for the past few years be utilized/consumed for the employer share contributions. Coordination with the HDMF personnel is to be done with regards to the foregoing recommendation.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Local Chief Executive issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order to create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team and direct the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the necessary status and evaluation reports for submission to DILG.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Lack of manpower for the MSWDO

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System and the Municipal Planning and Development Council initiate the establishment of GAD database and update the same in adherence to the provisions of RA 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Lack of manpower for the MSWDO

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management report accurately the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF to provide reliable and relevant information to the users. Further, the Management maximize the utilization of the fund to build disaster resilient communities and to reduce disaster risks at all levels in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the strengthening and implementation of PPAs under LCPC Fund. Further, consider additional planning and assessment tools in identifying PPAs as authorized under RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Unpaid Screen and Award Task Force, Office of the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Engineer, Ice Plant Operation Officer and Inventory Group to do their due duties and responsibilities to improve the operations of the Placer Integrated Transport and Food Terminal and the Ice Plant and to increase revenue thereof. Demand payment from the concerned stall occupants totaling ₱517,000.00 and require them to sign during the Contract of Lease. Propose to the Sangguniang Bayan for the enactment of an ordinance on fees and charges which are already collected by the LGU but no existing ordinance yet are enacted.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant conferred with the bank regarding the bank errors.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management:

Request the concerned officials and employees to liquidate/settle immediately their outstanding cash advances by demanding the submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports. Otherwise, withhold any money due them; and

Strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and full accounting of balances and avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances.

See reiterated finding no. 2.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to conduct verification, analysis and validation of these receivable balances, to determine their collectability and pursue collection, refund or liquidation thereof, if warranted. 

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive file the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts which could no longer be collected to the Commission on Audit, duly supported with the required documents.

Difficulty in providing the details of the balances and determining its validity.


Inability to provide the necessary documents to support the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the MGSO and Municipal Accountant prioritize the reconciliation of the results of the inventory count with the property and accounting records, look into the noted discrepancies and effect/prepare the necessary adjusting entries, as the case may be.


Likewise, the MGSO must prepare and maintain Stock and Property Cards to facilitate the required regular reconciliation of the PPE accounts. 


Moreover, the MGSO must conduct physical count and inspection of all PPE items including those which are unserviceable and obsolete and submit the RPCPPE and IIRUP with complete details. Then, immediately dispose those unserviceable and obsolete items so that they may be dropped from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The newly designated MGSO prioritized the preparation and submission of the RPCPPE.

Only those PPE which were constructed or purchased in the recent years were contained in the cards.

Management had conducted physical inventory taking but not all PPE. Auction of unserviceable items is being prepared.
observations.reason:
The submitted RPCPPE were still lacking of the required information. Also, reconciliation between the RPCPPE and General Ledgers were not made because of the delayed submission of the former.

Previous years’ PPE were not included in the Stock and Property Cards.

A new MGSO was designated, hence, some of the report, records and properties were not yet turned-over by the previous MGSO. 

See reiterated finding no. 1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Treasury Office and Budget Office, as the lead offices, and the Local Finance Committee study thoroughly the reasonableness of the projected  income to be collected to arrive at a more realistic appropriation; and

The Municipal Treasurer must conduct periodic review to check the collection performance level and devise remedial measures for revenue accounts that missed the projections and effective procedures that will improve more the collections of taxes that registered good results.

We recommended that Management:

Refund the amount of ₱120,000 for the double payment of the newspaper publication and furnish the Audit Team of the OR issued by the Municipal Treasurer on the refund;

Review and recompute thoroughly the documents supporting payments made to the supplier and demand refund from the supplier should it be established that an overpayment was made in addition to the double payment; and

Submit the lacking supporting documents and ensure its completeness before approving any disbursement voucher for payment.
observations.managementAction: Management sent a letter to the publisher regarding the possible double payment.
observations.reason: Management awaits for the reply of the publisher. Further, some of the lacking supporting documents were already submitted.

observations.reason: Notice of Suspension was issued pertaining to this AOM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officers and employees strictly adhere to the law and regulations in accounting and payment of obligation.  Appropriation ordinance of the current year must only be disbursed for expenditures incurred on that year.  Accordingly, the payment of diesel and gasoline subject of DV No. 101-20-07-1833 and Check No. 70516975 in the amount of ₱15,601 is required to be refunded immediately by officials/employees responsible for its authorization and payment pursuant to Section 351 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Refunded by Management in the amount of ₱15,601.00 under OR No. 6512549 dated December 31, 2021. NSSDC was issued on January 7, 2022.
observations.reason: Notice of Disallowance was issued pertaining to this AOM.

observations.managementAction: Management had reminded the LSB as to the prompt implementation of PPAs and inclusion of ECCD in the budget.
observations.reason: Management had reconstituted the LSB but implementation of the recommendation cannot be fully attained due to travel restrictions of DepEd personnel and lot of workloads.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU observe strictly the laws and regulations on the utilization of LDRRMF by utilizing the fund only for those allowed and legitimate PPAs to avoid depleting the fund for the expenditures outside the purpose for its creation.  
Explanation is also requested as to why Section 4(u) of RA No. 11494, DBM LBC No. 124, and Item 3 of GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 were not observed for the budgetary realignment of fund, procurements and disbursements, and the required posting to GPPB portal and other places.

We recommended that the LGU impose the liquidated damages incurred by the contractor in the construction of Drainage Canal Project.  Henceforth, strict compliance with the Procurement Law and its IRR is required for every procurement of the Municipality.  Accurate data and costing must be used in the preparation of Engineering Detailed Costs Estimated based on actual description of the project to avoid excessive and unnecessary costs leading to the formulation of the ABC which served as basis for bidders’ bid amount for the project.

observations.managementAction: Management had encouraged barangays to include the establishment of MRF in their budget.
observations.reason: Provision of MRF in every barangay cannot be fully implemented and construction of sanitary landfill due to insufficient fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person or its equivalent officers/employees to:

Strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations prescribing the guidelines in the formulation of Annual GPB, development of gender issues, GAD activities, and implementation and monitoring of the GPB.  Mainstreaming of regular PPAs must be made through the use of HGDG assessment tools to justify the attribution of the portion or whole costs to GAD budget; 

Avoid the general statements of the gender issue which do not necessarily reflect gender differences in the situation of women and men.  The gender issue must be specific and supported by evidence such as sex-disaggregated data and gender analysis results; and

Utilize the GAD Fund only for acceptable PPAs that would address the identified gender issues in the locality based on RA No. 9710 or the MCW, PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, and other relevant international commitments.
observations.managementAction: Management had committed to do their best efforts to comply with all the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Lack of proper training and knowledge on the use of HGDG assessment tool. Sex-disaggregated data and gender analysis was not regularly maintained and conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Implement immediately and on timely basis the identified PPAs under the 20 per cent DF.  The Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer must monitor the implementation of the projects to fast tract its completion;

The members of the BAC schedule early all procurements activities pertaining to development projects to pave way for their timely implementation and completion; and

All 20 per cent DF projects must be effectively planned and the same must be procurement-and implementation-ready.
observations.managementAction: Several PPAs are already completed and some are on-going.
observations.reason: Lack of monitoring on the implementation of the PPAs.

Delayed provision of the Municipal Assessor to the Municipal Treasurer of the list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed and the values of such properties, hence, the RPTAR was not updated by the Municipal Treasurer and the list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible was not furnished to the Municipal Accountant.

The Municipal Assessor submitted to the Municipal Treasurer an incomplete Assessment Roll and not within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish local guidelines on the grant of AICS and other financial assistance to indigent constituents which are in line with DSWD MC No. 04, series of 2015.

The Appraisal Committee to be led by the Municipal Assessor appraise the said properties to its current market value for the Municipal Accountant to record the same in the books in lieu of those with unidentified acquisition costs.
observations.managementAction: Verification of records and reconciliation between the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor is on-going.
observations.reason: The concerned offices were still in the process of reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure and submit immediately a specific legal basis for the grant of additional bonus to the municipal officials and employees for CY 2018 to avoid disallowance of said transaction.

observations.reason: Non-submission of specific legal basis for the grant of additional bonus.

Henceforth, the LSB shall strictly follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the aforementioned JC.
observations.managementAction: No Management action.

Update the existing tax rates, fees and charges prescribed in the Revenue Code to increase the revenue collection of local taxes and be able to sustain an adequate, appropriate and relevant tax rates in relation to the Municipality’s fast-paced growth and development requirements.

observations.reason: Not Implemented Reiterated in Finding No. 8

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source and implementing government entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of the balances; and to prepare aging of dormant fund transfers on a quarterly basis, using Annexes 1 to 3 of COA  Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, to support the request for write-off as provided in Sections 7.2 and 7.4 of said COA Circular. We recommended that Management establish and maintain a GAD Database containing gender statistics and age and sex-disaggregated data in compliance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to ensure that GAD PPAs address emerging and or continuing issues and concerns on the protection and fulfillment of women’s human rights, economic empowerment of women, and participation in local governance and decision-making.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Regularly conduct review and analysis of the records to determine the
nature of each account and come up with reconciled and correct balances
of the Cash in Bank accounts for all funds and reconcile with the records
of the depository banks;
35
b. Prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up all valid reconciling
items already determined as book error and to exert extra effort to
reconcile prior periods amounts which are still subject for verification;
c. Make written representation to the bank regarding the various bank
reconciling items not acted on. Henceforth, promptly inform the bank of
any error or discrepancy noted in the statement while the documents are
still available to ensure that correction is effected in the subsequent bank
statement; and
d. Prepare and submit the BRS, together with the copies of the bank
statements and supporting documents for the reconciling items, within the
prescribed period for a timely review/verification of such report.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management:
a. Request the concerned officials and employees to liquidate/settle
immediately their outstanding cash advances by demanding the submission
of duly accomplished liquidation reports. Otherwise, withhold any money
due them; and
b. Strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure
timely submission of liquidation documents and full accounting of balances
and avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to
conduct verification, analysis and validation of these receivable balances, to
determine their collectability and pursue collection, refund or liquidation thereof, if
warranted. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive file the request for
authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts which could no longer be collected
to the Commission on Audit, duly supported with the required documents.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the MGSO and
Municipal Accountant prioritize the reconciliation of the results of the inventory
count with the property and accounting records, look into the noted discrepancies
and effect/prepare the necessary adjusting entries, as the case may be.
Likewise, the MGSO must prepare and maintain Stock and Property Cards to
facilitate the required regular reconciliation of the PPE accounts.
Moreover, the MGSO must conduct physical count and inspection of all PPE
items including those which are unserviceable and obsolete and submit the RPCPPE
and IIRUP with complete details. Then, immediately dispose those unserviceable and
obsolete items so that they may be dropped from the books of accounts.

We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Treasury Office and Budget Office, as the lead offices, and
the Local Finance Committee study thoroughly the reasonableness of the
projected income to be collected to arrive at a more realistic
appropriation; and
b. The Municipal Treasurer must conduct periodic review to check the
collection performance level and devise remedial measures for revenue
accounts that missed the projections and effective procedures that will
improve more the collections of taxes that registered good results.

We recommended that Management:
a. Refund the amount of ₱120,000 for the double payment of the newspaper
publication and furnish the Audit Team of the OR issued by the Municipal
Treasurer on the refund;
b. Review and recompute thoroughly the documents supporting payments
made to the supplier and demand refund from the supplier should it be
established that an overpayment was made in addition to the double
payment; and
c. Submit the lacking supporting documents and ensure its completeness
before approving any disbursement voucher for payment.

We recommended that:
a. The LGU, through the concerned offices/departments, observe the prudent
fund management in its effort to curtail/eliminate/mitigate the spread of
COVID-19 in the locality. Henceforth, all PPAs related to COVID-19 must
be judiciously planned according to priorities considering the scarce
resources of the LGU and the urgent needs for health, safety, and
livelihood of the constituents;
b. Observe and adhere to the required posting of APP, NOA, and PPAs
implemented by the LGU to the GPPB portal for transparency and
reportorial compliance;
c. Satisfactory explanation is requested on the observations made in the
utilization and disbursement of BGCM Fund, otherwise transactions
covered by said observations will be suspended or disallowed in audit as
the case maybe; and
d. The MGSO, being the unit designated to receive all procurement and
subsequent distribution thereof, is requested to fully account and submit
inventory report on the food reliefs received, distributed and on hand.

We recommended that the LGU officers and employees strictly adhere to the
law and regulations in accounting and payment of obligation. Appropriation
ordinance of the current year must only be disbursed for expenditures incurred on
that year. Accordingly, the payment of diesel and gasoline subject of DV No. 101-20-
07-1833 and Check No. 70516975 in the amount of ₱15,601 is required to be refunded
immediately by officials/employees responsible for its authorization and payment
pursuant to Section 351 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the LSB:
1. Obtain/secure the required DepEd approved SIP, DEDP and other
information from the DepEd RO on the allocation of each school from the
National Budget as contained in the NEP, which supposedly were the basis
for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget and
furnish the same to the Audit Team, for reference and review purposes.
Henceforth, the LSB must strictly follow the mandated required processes
and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF;
2. Incorporate in the budget programs and activities intended for ECCD as
required under Section 7 (b) of RA No. 10410;
3. Implement immediately all programmed PPAs under the approved SEF
budget for the ultimate benefits of the recipients thereof. Explanation is
requested as to why all those programmed PPAs were not implemented
during CY 2020 while the constituents are needing them most; and
4. Adhere strictly on the applicable accounting policy in the recognition of
asset acquired under the fund. The asset acquired in CY 2019 and charged
against the current budget must be rectified immediately to avoid audit
disallowance thereof.

We recommended that the LGU observe strictly the laws and regulations on
the utilization of LDRRMF by utilizing the fund only for those allowed and legitimate
PPAs to avoid depleting the fund for the expenditures outside the purpose for its
creation. Explanation is also requested as to why Section 4(u) of RA No. 11494, DBM
LBC No. 124, and Item 3 of GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 were not observed for the
budgetary realignment of fund, procurements and disbursements, and the required
posting to GPPB portal and other places.

We recommended that the LGU impose the liquidated damages incurred by
the contractor in the construction of Drainage Canal Project. Henceforth, strict
compliance with the Procurement Law and its IRR is required for every procurement
73
of the Municipality. Accurate data and costing must be used in the preparation of
Engineering Detailed Costs Estimated based on actual description of the project to
avoid excessive and unnecessary costs leading to the formulation of the ABC which
served as basis for bidders’ bid amount for the project.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Create and organize new set of SWMB;
b. Strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on “no
segregation, no collection” policy among residents and consider imposing
fines and penalties for violators thereof;
c. Encourage and provide needed assistance to the remaining 33 barangays to
establish their own MRFs for the collection, segregation, recycling of
biodegradable, recyclable, compostable and reusable wastes;
d. Allocate funds for the construction and operation of a sanitary landfill as a
final disposal site for solid wastes; and
e. Review the reports submitted and explain or account for the difference of
the amounts utilized per progress report of the SWM Focal Person and the
accomplishment report of the 20 per cent Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal
Person or its equivalent officers/employees to:
a. Strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations prescribing the
guidelines in the formulation of Annual GPB, development of gender
issues, GAD activities, and implementation and monitoring of the GPB.
Mainstreaming of regular PPAs must be made through the use of HGDG
assessment tools to justify the attribution of the portion or whole costs to
GAD budget;
b. Avoid the general statements of the gender issue which do not necessarily
reflect gender differences in the situation of women and men. The gender
issue must be specific and supported by evidence such as sex-disaggregated
data and gender analysis results; and
c. Utilize the GAD Fund only for acceptable PPAs that would address the
identified gender issues in the locality based on RA No. 9710 or the MCW,
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, and other
relevant international commitments.

We recommended the following:
a. Implement immediately and on timely basis the identified PPAs under the
20 per cent DF. The Municipal Planning and Development Officer and
Municipal Engineer must monitor the implementation of the projects to
fast tract its completion;
b. The members of the BAC schedule early all procurements activities
pertaining to development projects to pave way for their timely
implementation and completion; and
c. All 20 per cent DF projects must be effectively planned and the same must
be procurement-and implementation-ready.

12.6	Furthermore, for the meantime, stop paying for the employer share of the employees until such time that the excess payments for the past few years be utilized/consumed for the employer share contributions. Coordination with the HDMF personnel is to be done with regards to the foregoing recommendation.

observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management:

Request the concerned officials and employees to liquidate/settle immediately their outstanding cash advances by demanding the submission of duly accomplished liquidation reports. Otherwise, withhold any money due them; 

Review the non-moving balances and take appropriate action to ensure liquidation of the cash advances. If settlement is not possible, the same must be requested for write-off subject to the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005; and

Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances for a more efficient and effective internal control system.
observations.managementAction: Close monitoring of cash advances is being done and demand letters were sent to concerned officials and employees.
observations.reason: Unavailability of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough verification of dormant items, and devise effective measures to ensure collection of receivables and settlement of payables. Otherwise, if proven futile, request for the write-off of those accounts from the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005. We further recommended that a regular review of all account balances be conducted so that dormant accounts can immediately be identified and acted upon.
observations.managementAction: Thorough verification of dormant items was done and due to absence of records and documents to validate/ support the claims, balances could not be ascertained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the lacking supporting documents and observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and 2015-009 on the documentation requirements and proper recording of fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: The utilization of funds were monitored through their disbursements only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken will be in accordance with the approved APP and prepare and update the consolidated APP at least every six months or as often as may be required by the Head of the Procuring Entity.

We recommended that Management require the national government employees to furnish Authority to Travel duly signed and approved by their heads or supervisors of their offices, departments or bureau to support their claims of travel allowances. Likewise, complete documentation must be observed in the reimbursement of travel expenses by the personnel.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer must make sure that all paid vouchers are transmitted promptly to the Office of the Accountant. On the other hand, the Office of the Municipal Accountant must ensure timely and complete submission of all DVs within the period prescribed under 7.2.1a of COA Circular 2009-006 and Section 44 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Management require the Local School Board (LSB) to strictly comply with the requirements of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 in identifying the PPAs that reflect an equitable distribution of the mandated priority expenses and maximize the implementation of the identified programs to ensure efficient utilization of the fund so that its objectives are fully achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management organize and establish immediately the LDRRMO in accordance with the provisions set forth in RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC - DILG - DBM - CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014 - 01 dated April 4, 2014 so that efforts will be coordinated in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management towards building disaster-resilient local communities.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management strengthen and hasten the full implementation of PPAs funded out of the 20 per cent Development Fund to achieve the desirable outcomes from the delivery of the social, economic and environmental services to the constituents.
observations.reason: CY 2017 PPAs charged under the 20 per cent development fund were already implemented but the same issue were brought up again in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent Development Fund is used solely for socio/economic development projects as well as environmental management and no amount is utilized for expenditure items not allowed to be charged under the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Subsequent disbursements charged to the 20 per cent development fund were already checked.

observations.recommendation:

Also, submit the approved Annual Work and Financial Plan and Accomplishment Report to facilitate monitoring of the accomplishment vis-à-vis the targeted projects for effective and efficient utilization of the fund.

Otherwise, any remittance in excess of ₱100.00 per month should be charged to the employees’ personal share.

We recommended that Management:

Strictly adhere to the requirement that all payments must be made by check in the name of the supplier/contractor/payee pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

1. Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Accountant’s Office to reconcile the entries in the Cashbook and Subsidiary/General Ledger (SL/GL) at least quarterly to avoid accumulation of variance and submit the adjusting entries on the reconciled amounts on collections and deposits for calendar year 2017.

2. Municipal Accountant’s Office to submit immediately all the lacking SL for the Cash-Local Treasury account as of December 31, 2017

3. Revenue Collectors to turn over the collections daily to the Liquidating Officer and the latter to deposit immediately her undeposited collections as of  to avoid possible loss of government funds and the LGU could use the fund for public purpose.

We recommended that Management submit immediately the amortization schedule and photocopies of paid vouchers on all previous loan repayments to the Office of the Auditor as required in Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and reconcile their records with the MDFO to ascertain the correctness of the Loans Payable-Domestic account balance. Direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office to prepare adjusting journal entries to record the payments of P644,000.00 to MDFO charged against the 20% DF and not from the DBP Proper (80% of the IRA) by debiting CIB-LCCA (DBP Proper) and crediting CIB-LCCA (20% DF).

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant’s Office to reconcile the difference between the GL and SL of the Due to NGAs account in the General Fund amounting to P1,371,022.48 and to reclassify the accounts used on transactions in the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs account totaling P1,431,543.21.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office to retrieve the source documents on RPT share of the Province of Surigao del Norte and various barangays of LGU Placer and the corresponding remittances, and effect adjusting entries duly supported with documents to arrive accurate balance of Due to LGUs on RPT and submit the supporting documents for adjusting journal entries in the amount of P704, 070.47.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for all transactions involving Assistance to Disadvantage Municipalities Fund as provided for in Section 4.3.1 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3 dated May 23, 2017.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant’s Office:

1. To maintain separate SLs for transfers of LGU’s unutilized DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund such as CIB-LCCA -LDRRMF (Special Trust Fund)/ and Trust Liabilities –DRRMF by year of transfer; and

2. To disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details                       of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF using the format in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management direct:

1. The MDRRM Council prepare the LDRRMFIP 30% allocation for QRF in lump sum and in details as to projects and activities to be funded by the 70% allocation for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery, and to incorporate the projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund using the format of Annex A of Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and



2. The Municipal Accountant’s Office, MDRRM Office, MDRRM Council and Municipal Development Council to prepare and submit the monthly and annual Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Auditor using the format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management direct the:
1. Municipal Accountant’s Office to promptly prepare the disbursement voucher on the remittance of Provincial share from the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property accruing to the SEF; and

2. Municipal Treasurer’s Office to effect the payment thereof within fifteen (15) days of every succeeding month and remit immediately the unreleased shares as of December 31, 2017 in compliance with Section 9 of Republic Act No. 5447.

We recommended that Management direct the members of the LCPC and Municipal Social Welfare Development Office:

1. To closely monitor and implement the PPAs for LCPC and to maximize the utilization of the LCPC fund; and 

2. To prepare Children Profile and to establish data base on children for proper planning and identification of PPAs which is the most need of the children in the Municipality for full delivery of the services as enumerated in Section II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that Management direct the local officials concerned to immediately install the community billboard as prescribed in Annex D of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-3 dated May 23, 2017 and in compliance with Section 2.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 13, 2013 for transparency and information of the public.

We recommended:
1. That the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% development fund and fast-track the implementation of the unimplemented and on-going development projects as of December 31, 2017; and
2. The Municipal Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to closely monitor and strongly pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs and projects of the 20% DF and to regularly submit status reports to the Municipal Mayor so that appropriate measures can be instituted.

We recommended that Management optimally use the 70% allocation of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund through the implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and Municipal Environment and Natural Resource Office to develop specific mechanics and guidelines to properly implement the Solid Waste Management PPAs of LGU Placer and oversee its implementation to fully utilize the fund of P634,401.95 for timely benefits that the public could derived therefrom.

We recommend that Management establish and maintain a GAD Database containing gender statistics and age and sex-disaggregated data in compliance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to ensure that GAD PPAs address emerging and or continuing issues and concerns on the protection and fulfillment of women’s human rights, economic empowerment of women, and participation in local governance and decision-making.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to periodically review and analyze all prior years and current year’s transactions involving reciprocal accounts and effect adjust-ments in the respective books of accounts to ensure that all financial trans-actions are properly taken up in the books. Likewise, we recommend that Management record its financial transactions and operations conformably with generally accepted accounting principles and in accordance with pertinent laws and regulations as provided for in Section 112 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommend that the accountable officers concerned strictly comply with the regulations on the liquidation of cash advances as mentioned in Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  Otherwise, failure of the AO to liquidate his cash advance within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of his salary as provided for in Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and the instruction of other sanctions as provided for under paragraph 9.2 and 9.3 thereof. Likewise, failure to liquidate the cash advances in aggregate amounts of Fifty Thousand Pesos (Php50,000.00) and above per accountable officer within the period prescribed shall constitute administrative offense of Gross Neglect of Duty or Simple Neglect of Duty, as the case may be, pursuant to CSC Resolution No. 1200103 dated January 12, 2012. Further, we also recommend the Municipal Accountant to include all cash advances of special disbursing officers, disbursing officers and officials and employees in the Monthly and Quarterly Monitoring Report of Cash Advances.

We recommend that Management compel the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the RCI daily for each fund and submit the same together with the DVs and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant as provided for in Section 43 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the said report and supporting documents to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Otherwise, their failure or refusal to submit papers relative to government obligations is a ground for disciplinary action and disallowance of the transactions as provided for in Section 39 (3) of P.D. No. 1445 and failure to render accounts for a period of two months after such accounts should be rendered is penalized in Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommend also that Management enroll with the LANDBANK we Access which offers a wide range of institutional banking transactions to generate immediate information.

We recommend that Management cause the automatic suspension of the payment of the accountable officers’ salaries and other emoluments until they shall have complied with the submission of the financial statements and schedules as provided for in Section 41 (3) of PD 1445. Compel the Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly and Quarterly Pre-Closing Trial Balance   not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month duly supported by the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, for both the current and continuing appropriations and supported by a schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts in the General Ledger; and the Monthly BRS for all depository bank accounts maintained by LGU within ten (10) days from receipt of the Bank Statements. Likewise, submit the same together with the Bank Statements and paid checks to the Audit Team.

We recommend also that Management enroll with the LANDBANK we Access which offers a wide range of institutional banking transactions to generate immediate information.

We recommend that Management direct the Solid Waste Management Board to require all barangays of LGU-Placer to allocate a certain parcel of land through its Sanggunian for the establishment of MRF. Direct the Punong Barangays to comply the guidelines and criteria set in Section 33 of RA 9003 in the establishment of MRF to accommodate efficient and safe materials processing, movement, and storage; and to allow efficient and safe external access and to accommodate internal flow. We recommend that Local Chief Executive to close the open dump site and have a sanitary landfill site in accordance with Sections 41 and 42 to protect personnel and public health and safety.

We recommend that Management compel the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant. Require the MDRRMO to submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRM Council and Local Development Council to the COA auditor of the LGU.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive expedite the implementation of projects, programs and activities so that intended recipients of the fund transfers could immediately benefit from it. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Checks Issued and the Report of Disbursement with all supporting vouchers/payrolls and documents within           five (5 days) after the end of each month to the Accountant. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Reports to the Auditor of the LGU and to the Source Agency (SA) (Attention: The SA Accountant) within ten (10) days after receipt from the AO as mentioned in COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994. Further, we recommend the Management to submit subsidiary ledgers in the quarterly pre-closing trial balance and year-end financial statements and the MOA/TA to the Auditor within five (5) working days from the execution of a contract to evaluate whether the fund is utilized within the agreed stipulations.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF to help achieve prompt desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommend that Management manage and immediately utilize the fund efficiently and effectively to ensure that the residents and the public schools within the Municipality could benefit from the PPAs of the SEF.

We recommend that Management optimally utilize the LCPC to fully uphold the essence of the institutionalization of LCPC to advocate child rights, plans and initiates/ recommends interventions and monitors children’s programs and projects in the locality. We recommend also that Management prepare Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to the needs for integration in the local development plan and Local State of Children’s Report as mentioned in Section II of DILG Memorandum Circular.

We recommend that Management create the GAD Focal Point System and establish GAD Database to accelerate gender mainstreaming and to have adequate information for gender-responsiveness, programming and policy making using Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. We recommend also that management maximize the utilization of the GAD Fund to support the policy of the State as provided for in Section 2 of PD 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC of the Municipal Government should adhere strictly with the provisions of Section 12.2 and shall prepare Procurement Monitoring Report in its prescribed format for the two semesters of CY 2015 for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to GPPB.

We recommend that Management reprimand and direct the concerned officials & employees to immediately submit the lacking reports and every reglementary period, thereafter. Otherwise, pursuant to Sec.144 (2) of PD 1445, suspension of payment of their salaries may be imposed.

We recommend that Management compel the Property Officer, Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to adhere the provisions of Custodianship and Issuance and Utilization in the Training Handbook on Property and Supply Management System. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality for inventory-taking is an indispensable procedure for checking the integrity of property custodianship. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) upon completion of the physical count and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year. Reconcile the said report with the accounting and property records. Moreover, instruct the Property Officer to renew ARE every January after the third (3rd) year of issue. Require all accountable officers issued with equipment to return the unserviceable property to the Property Officer and let the latter prepare the IIRUP for the Inspection Officer to validate such condition with the presence of the Auditor concerned.
